description: Item-property taxonomy for Tier 3 semantic coherence testing via Fisher's exact test

P1_entity_count.description: Number of entities in the task (3 vs 5)
P1_entity_count.rationale: Cleanest structural split. 5-entity tasks require more computation and have lower baseline.

P2_task_type.description: Task family: tracking (state update) vs deduction (constraint satisfaction)
P2_task_type.rationale: Tests whether intervention effects are reasoning-type-specific.

P3_swap_count.description: Number of swap/switch operations in tracking tasks (low vs high)
P3_swap_count.rationale: Tests whether interventions differentially affect items requiring more sequential state updates.

P4_input_length.description: Input text length tercile (short vs long, within each task)

split.short: bottom tercile of character count within task
split.long: top tercile of character count within task

P4_input_length.rationale: Control property. Weak theoretical motivation but tests positional encoding effects.

P5_queried_position.description: Whether the question asks about a boundary vs interior entity (deduction tasks only)

split.boundary: question asks about first or last position

P5_queried_position.rationale: Boundary positions are harder; tests whether interventions help/hurt constraint propagation.

P6_answer_position.description: Whether the correct answer is the first option (A) vs last option (C or E)

split.last_option: correct answer is (C) for 3-obj or (E) for 5-obj

P6_answer_position.rationale: Near-zero baseline gap makes any post-intervention divergence diagnostic of positional bias changes.

testing_protocol.application: For each Tier 2 result, compute flip rates within each property group and test for non-random distribution of flipped items across groups.
testing_protocol.table_construction: 2x2 table: rows = property group (A vs B), columns = flipped vs not-flipped. 'Flipped' means the item changed correctness (either direction) between baseline and intervention.
